what_it_does: Acceptance test that exercises Suite._design_critic as the live, in-repo machine half of the FORM gate. It plants temporary surface files, runs the real design-lint corpus over changed files, and asserts pass/fail/fail-safe/no-form outcomes for backend, clean token-only, off-token, missing-file, missing-corpus, pure-logic .ts, and mixed .tsx+.ts cases.
observations: Uses the real in-repo design-lint script (design/_system/check.py) rather than a mock.
Every surface file is planted in a fresh temporary sandbox so the real repo canvas/ is never touched.
Defines CLEAN_TSX and DIRTY_TSX fixtures to contrast token-only var(--x) usage with raw #hex and rgba(...) literals.
A pure-backend build with no canvas/ change passes because there is no form to grade.
A clean token-only surface change passes and is described as eligible for auto-close.
An off-token surface change with raw color literals fails the gate.
Pre-existing dirt elsewhere is ignored because the gate only lints the named changed files.
Fail-safe cases (missing surface file, missing corpus lint script) return False and are never silent True.
A pure-logic .ts canvas file is treated as having no lintable form and passes.
Mixed .tsx + .ts change sets follow the .tsx verdict; the .ts does not flip the outcome.
standouts: Integration with the real corpus lint script makes this an end-to-end gate acceptance test, not a unit test of a stub.
Explicit fail-safe semantics: unverifiable always returns False with a fail-safe label.
Isolation of the changed-file set is tested directly by planting dirty files outside the change set.
Distinguishes a missing styled file (fail-safe) from a no-form .ts file (pass).
conventions: Global PASS counter incremented by a check() assertion helper.
Long module docstring states intent, run command, and scope.
Relative surface paths mirror a real repo layout under canvas/app/src.
Subclass injection (_NoCorpus) simulates an external failure condition without mutating the real repo.
concerns: Global PASS counter and check() side effects are not thread-safe and mix assertion logic with bookkeeping.
Temp directories created by fresh_suite() are not cleaned up, so runs may leave directories behind.
Test 0 checks that the corpus script exists but does not prove it is the same path Suite will resolve at runtime.
Test 7's claim that pure-logic .ts has no lintable form depends on Suite._design_critic behavior not visible in this file.
Repeated reassignment of s and base shadows prior suites but is harmless because each uses a new temp directory.
notes: An acceptance test that proves the machine FORM gate is live, fail-safe, and correctly classifies token-only, off-token, no-form, and unverifiable surface changes using temporary files and the real design-lint corpus.
questions: What exact lint rules does design/_system/check.py enforce beyond raw hex/rgba detection?
How does Suite._design_critic decide whether a file is a styled surface (.tsx/.css) versus a no-form .ts file?
Where and how is the separate human-judgment browser stage dispatched at verify time?
Are the temporary directories created by fresh_suite() expected to be cleaned up, and if so, by what mechanism?
What is the intended lifecycle of the FsStore and NodeRegistry instances created for each throwaway suite?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it demonstrates by use that Suite._design_critic is a real machine FORM gate, covers pass/fail/fail-safe/no-form cases, and uses the real in-repo lint corpus on isolated temp files.
apparent_themes: Separation of machine lint from human visual judgment
intention_signals: The file is trying to graduate the FORM gate from a hard-coded stub into a live, machine-enforced design critic that auto-approves clean token surfaces, blocks off-token or unverifiable surfaces, and leaves human visual judgment to a later verify-time stage.
novelty: It treats the design-lint corpus as a build gate integrated into Suite._design_critic, with explicit fail-safe semantics and a temp-tree acceptance harness that runs the real corpus script rather than mocking it.
contribution: A behavioral contract and acceptance harness for the machine half of the FORM gate, documenting and enforcing expected outcomes for clean, off-token, no-form, and unverifiable surface changes.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for Suite._design_critic that validates the in-repo machine FORM gate using temporary surface files and the real design-lint corpus.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the FORM gate is live, fail-safe, and correctly classifies token-only, off-token, no-form, and unverifiable surface changes.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a pass counter and prints ok for each verified condition.
symbols.description: Global check function used throughout the test module. It asserts the supplied condition, increments the PASS counter on success, and prints a success line; failure raises an AssertionError with the given label.

symbols.does: Creates a Suite instance rooted in a fresh temporary directory.
symbols.description: Builds a throwaway repo sandbox with an empty nodes directory, an FsStore, and a NodeRegistry, then returns the Suite and the temp base path so tests never modify the real repo canvas/.

symbols.does: Writes a surface file into the sandbox at a relative path.
symbols.description: Creates parent directories as needed and writes the provided body to <repo_root>/<rel>, simulating a build side effect on a surface file such as a .tsx component.

symbols.does: Suite subclass that reports a non-existent design-lint corpus path.
symbols.description: Test-only subclass used to simulate a missing design-lint corpus so the fail-safe behavior of _design_critic can be exercised without deleting or altering the real in-repo script.

symbols.does: Returns a bogus missing script path to trigger the fail-safe path.
symbols.description: Overrides Suite's corpus locator to point at a temporary non-existent file, causing _design_critic to treat the surface as unverifiable and return False with a fail-safe reason.
